sentences.text: Hello?
Hey everyone, I'll get my camera turned on.
There we go.
Good morning!
Morning, how is everyone?
Good, how are you?
I'm doing well.
How's your morning going so far?
It's a busy Monday, but that's alright.
Are they all busy in your world, though?
Yeah, Mondays are… are never easy.
We appreciate you spending your 30 minutes of your Monday with us.
Yeah, of course.
Where are you guys all located?
We are all in the Madison, Wisconsin area.
Okay, awesome.
Yeah.
Ever been?
I've been to Madison, it's been a long time, but I spent a decade in Chicago before I moved down to Texas.
Nice, yeah, I was looking… I saw you're a Valpo guy, right?
Is that where you went to college?
Yep, went to Valpo.
Nice.
I grew up in Indiana.
time ago.
I grew up in the South Bend, Indiana area, so… Kind of close with the Valpo.
Yeah, right down the road.
Yeah.
Hey, Saad.
What's up, guys?
Yo!
Hurry on.
Good, how are you?
I'm good, sorry I was a little late wrapping up a call before this.
All look good.
Well, we can start by introducing ourselves.
Yeah.
First time meeting, David.
I'm Kylie, been working with the asset team for a few years now.
I've been with the company, oh, it just hit 7 years.
So my role within the company is working with, like, our largest companies across the country for our Maven AI product.
And I'm Morgan.
Nice to meet you.
I'm on the customer success team.
I've been with this company for 5 years, also working with the asset portfolio for pretty much a majority of that, about 4 of those 5.
And I also work on our largest accounts, our enterprise accounts, mainly on this Maven AI product as well.
So I'm managing the account and all of the reporting and anything that you guys need day-to-day.
I'm Patrick.
Lead the sales team, David.
We met very briefly at NAA.
We're… this is the same company that's, as DepartmentIQ, so we met very briefly at NAA, but I lead the sales team for Maven.
Been here 9 years, so… Cool to see the company grow, and it was nice to get to know Saad a little bit.
We had a nice dinner at AIM, and excited to talk to you again today, David.
Excellent, yeah, great to meet you all.
Yeah, we'd, … when we were looking at AIQ, we'd talked about Maven, and it was always kind of… on the back burner, but I… so it's nice to kind of see what it actually does now.
Yeah, I remember that.

And it's, … we'll show you today, but the product has grown a lot in the last 18 months, so great timing to be talking to you, and obviously asset has grown significantly as well, so exciting stuff all around.
Yeah, that's great.
And I guess I should… I should introduce myself.
So, I've been with Asset… About 8 years now.
Right now, I've served a number of roles, but right now, I'm really working on strategic partnerships in any way that those come.
So, work with Saad and Alex on various marketing products, work with you know, other folks in the organization on… in other categories.
But, yeah, glad to learn more.
You guys all know me, but again, thank you guys for taking the time to meet, you know, early part of the week.
Really, David's the most important person.
Kylie, you and I already briefed last week on kind of how I would like the call structure.

David, for your benefit, and feel free to obviously pivot the call as you need, but… I figured I'd just do a quick 5-minute demo, and I already shared the deck and everything with you, but just do a quick 5-minute run-through on the product itself.
But then, really, the key things that I wanted to talk about personally is the support from their team on implementation as we scale this product.

And then the second thing is obviously pricing, figuring out, you know, what that looks like, and if it's a tiered approach, or if it's just a, you know, one-size-fits-all approach, whatever it may be.
But, those are really the three things that I wanted to cover on this call particularly, and this may be one of two calls, right?
First one, and you guys get back to us with.
kind of how you would take that approach, and then, we'll review the deck and proposal, and then maybe we'll set up another call.
But, you know, I think right now we've got.

I'm probably gonna butcher this, but I know it's upwards of 50-plus accounts already, and Phoenix team is bringing over, like, 60, 70 accounts that they want to bring on with you guys, so… so we're definitely scaling at a really rapid pace now, after we've test piloted with you guys on the new development side for the most part, so… really, I don't think there was ever a more perfect time to start having this conversation.

And my biggest piece, outside of pricing, which obviously benefits everybody, is that support component, because the last thing I want is whenever we scale with any partner, is for us to have more tasks being taken on our end, or there's a fallout or dropout in service levels or implementation levels.

That's the easiest way to lose buy-in and trust across the board when we were a lot of partners, so I want a heavy emphasis on that, selfishly, and just from a… long-term, you know, approach of how this product can help us be successful.
So, with that, I'll shut up, and you guys are the four most important people on this call.
I'll just look here and sit here and look pretty, so….
Doing a great job of that.
I try.
Thanks, Ed.
Well, after the chat that we had on Friday, we put a little slide deck together, but thinking for the call today, talk… quick recap of the product.
I know it sounds like you guys have chatted already, and gone through maybe a deck or the product, but just a quick overview on the product itself.
Go over the properties that we do have live.
We did just add another, group with the Phoenix team.
And then talk about onboarding, what that looks like, and then MSA pricing and terms.
So….
Perfect.
Jumping into, like, the background of the product, so… every product that we have under the Maven name is really built to automate processes that your teams are doing right now.
So, completely automate the entire process of posting to the Google profiles, the social profiles.
And really streamlining the whole process, optimizing your profiles in a really hands-off way.
So we have direct integrations with your property management softwares, and then we have an internal tool that we've built, that uses AI to come up with captions and content to post on your profiles.
A quick jump into the products that we have under the Maven AI name.
The one that we have the most properties on with asset is our Google Business Automation Tool.
But we do also have an automated social tool, that similar concept to the Google tool, uses automation and AI to post on your property profiles, your Instagram and Facebook pages.
We did have that, the Phoenix Group roll this out for the 60 that they just onboarded with us.
And then we had some conversations, around different teams about our reputation and our listings management tools as well.
So, those are the four products under the Maven name, but for the sake of the conversation today, we can focus on the Google tool, and maybe a little bit of the social.
Hi, and if you don't mind just briefly touching on the listings management as well.
Reputation management, full transparency, obviously we have opinion, kind of nationally, and then we do have some stats on Jay Turner as well, primarily using our student housing platform.

But those are the three, you know, obviously we would have 3 different tiered pricing that we want to come up with, but the all-inclusive package, or whatever the case is, but just a little bit into listings management as well would be great.
Okay.
Yeah, the listings tool, so Google and social, those two tools are built in-house, those are our products.
We do white-label BirdEye for listings management.
So we met with Shelby and her team on that tool a couple weeks back.
Just to get… just to get a demo in front of them and show them what that product is.
But we can definitely look at roping that in with a package bundle, if that's what you're looking for.
Listing side, just very briefly, is syncing your property-level information, phone, hours of operation, address across Google, Apple, Bing, Siri, all of the directories at one time.
Yelp being one.
So, that's… something that we offer, as it kind of is adjacent to our Google Business Automation.
Yeah.
So….
Who else is doing the Google business management in terms of who do you compete with?
Do any of our other providers also bundle that into packages?
I don't think I've seen that from anybody else, but I'm curious where you've run into it.
I think you all had… maybe had something with rent in the past that did some Google Business automation, and they got swallowed up by FLO recently.

I don't know to what scale you work with Zumper, but Zumper is an ILS that does They, part… they white-label a company called Digible for Google posts, so they do some Google business automation as well, and those are kind of the two.
Out there.
Okay.
Got it.
Yeah, we don't use a lot of zumper, a little bit, but pretty small.
Yup.
their main product is the ILS, and they try to bolt on, kind of, the Google side, too.
Yep.

Well, before we jump into, like, an overview of the Google tool, just to show a little bit of the scale of, of Maven over the last year, we went from 200,000 units over one year to just hit 2 million units.
So, 2 million units that we're partnering with on our Google Business automation tool specifically.
We show the slide to portfolios of your size, just to, like, we can support scale, with you all.
I know David and Saad, you talked, which makes sense, massive portfolio.
a lot of AI companies promise efficiencies, but the onboarding process is a… it's really hard.

So we show this to say we've built and scaled this and are continuing to grow, and can support portfolios of your size, and want to walk through how we could possibly do that on onboarding and implementation.
So, it sounds like you may have seen a high level of this already, but just wanted to put one slide in to show the product itself.

So, the Google Business tool, like I said, it's built to fully automate and optimize your profiles, automate processes your teams are already doing, and then increase exposure for your profiles by consistent optimization of the profiles.
So we're doing that in a few different areas throughout the profile itself.
The tool includes daily automated posts.
So, the source of truth for all of the information that we're pulling is your feed.
So we have that direct feed syndication, pulling amenity-based information, photos, directly from your feed to your profile, in a fully automated way.
Same thing with the products feed.
That would be the live pricing and availability feed, that is displaying current availability on your profile, direct from your feed to your profile.
Again, all automated, updating a few times every day to make sure that information is fresh, updated, and accurate.

We're also able to pull concession data, so pulling concession data, again, directly from the feed to the profile, and having that be… it's a live, like, dynamic post that we make, so we're able to have that removed as soon as that concession's removed, so we're not storing any outdated, old concessions.
And then fully automated Q&A.

Had a lot of customers over the last few months tell us that they're increasingly looking at the importance of FAQ type of content on their websites, because AI overviews really like that type of content.
So, this is the same thing, just getting that same FAQ information live on… right on your Google profile.
And Kyle, you guys, you guys, work with every property management software, right?
Yeah, we do.
I mean, all the major ones.
Yeah.
Yep.
Any, any, … it's mostly RealPage for you all, Saad?
Is that right?
We're actually mostly Yardi now.
Oh, you are.
Yeah.
But you already RealPage and Entrata.
Yep.
Yeah, those are the big three.
Rent Cafe, The RealPage Syndication, Entrata, if you have a random resmin out there, like, work with them too, so all the main syndication feeds.
I'm just… so I'm just pulling up a couple of our properties that use this, because I'm curious.
… Okay, I do see the products down on… The bottom of the page, okay.
I was wondering where those… where the pricing per unit shows up, and I do see that here.
Yep, with the student properties, we got a little creative to match the per-bed pricing, and then in that products feed, we also just added the ability to post amenities in there as well.
So some of the properties, you may be seeing amenity keywords with a matching photo, that's coming through our feed as well.
Yeah, that's what I was showing, you know, the community space and clubhouse, and then, then the units after that.
Yep.

Yeah, David, not to oversimplify the product, but we take the data and information that lives on your website, and that's on your ILSs, and we populate it all over your profile, automatically, on Google.
So, more and more content on Google from restaurants and hotels and local businesses can be from the website can now be on Google, and we're kind of the conduit to do that for you.
Yep, so overview of the current partnership, we have 63 conventional, 61 of those were just added with the Phoenix team, and we're meeting them tomorrow to go over, onboarding.
And then we have 26 from the new dev team.
10, new dev student properties, and then 7 student properties.
So, after the rest of these get onboarded, we'll be at 106 properties live.
And then, I know topic of the conversation today is about implementation, so, our average implementation time for the group that we have on now is about 5 business days.
And Kylie, what exactly is the process?
Because I haven't been part of the actual implementation.
Do you mind just giving a brief overview?
Like, what exactly is it that our team is having to do, and what do you need?

Like, how can we find efficiencies there if we scale, you know, bigger and bigger, like, … just kind of thinking from a bandwidth standpoint, you know, like, say we end up bringing in 200 properties all of a sudden once this is all agreed upon.
Like, what does that workload look like for my team currently on the marketing side, and their involvement?
Yeah, we definitely have the bandwidth for it.
The process right now… let me skip forward a little bit… the process right now is… Your team is reaching out to us with a property that they want to add.
I'm sending over a contract for every property, and then when that contract is signed, then your team just has two steps.
So the steps are turning the feed on.
So, turning the feed on to allow us to grab the information, and then allowing us Google management access.
So, we do that through providing a group code.
That group code gets inserted, and then we have management access.
So those are really… other than the contract right now, those are the two steps to give us all the information we need, and then our team takes over from there.
Cool.
Yeah, so for those 60 properties, we put all 60 in, like, an Exhibit A, and it was, like, it wasn't 60 separate agreements, it was an agreement with 60.

I think one of the things we wanted to talk to you today is… is ways that we have streamlined the contract signing process after an MSA is in place, which, of course, we'll have to iron out those details.
For portfolios in….
you know, similar scale, not quite as large.
We've streamlined and kind of cut out the… every single individual contract stage so that it goes a little faster, if that's something you'd be open to.
Yeah, we're definitely open to that structure when we get to this type of scale, having an MSA in place with an Exhibit A that we just update instead of signing individual contracts.
Live Exhibit A software of choice is smart.
Smartsheet, any experience with Smartsheet, David, Inside, in the world?
Yeah, I haven't used it specifically for contracts for Exhibit A, but I've used it for several other, projects.
It's great.
Sorry, I kind of interrupted you, go ahead.
Oh, you're good.
Yeah, we have… so, our biggest customers that have rolled this out to this scale, have really liked the Smartsheet.
They've told us that they think it's a pretty seamless way, of entering property information.
What we did with another customer was got the MSA in place that just showed a price per property.
And then the Smartsheet is their team's inputting the property name, the address, checking that they turned the feed on, checking that they gave management access.
And then the Smartsheet, it's a live sheet, so I get pinged, Morgan gets pinged, and then our implementation team gets pinged, too.
So it really cuts out a lot of time in between of having to wait for the contract signature, wait for the information.
It's been a really good process, and it seems like it's been the easiest way of going about this.
Great.
Yeah, if you could send me the order form with the 60 properties as a starting place, that would be great.
Yep.
Yeah, I'll get that over to you after this.
Any other questions on this piece of it?
I know this was something you wanted to focus on, Saad, was expedited onboarding, how to make this easiest on all ends.
Good on my end, David.
Any further questions on this particular end?
No, not right now.
Alright.

I think what happened with Phoenix was Lindsay and team, were interested in the product, wanted to do it, and then I think the implementation is rolling through Christie Foster, who saw it on your team.
So, imagine when we get to the point of, if we did the Smartsheet, the… somebody like Christie would be the one maybe inputting and handling the… properties that are added to the sheet.
Obviously.
So….
through that.
Yeah, it's, so we have a unique structure here.
Not every… not every property has a dedicated marketing manager, right?

So, if they're going through a centralized supporting ticketing system, then you'll have a different team member, or two working through that, so… But in the cases of the Christie's of the world, or, you know, Shelby has, like, the Noah's and Serenas, and all those people that you guys have met, right?

They will work directly, but… a lot of our properties don't have that dedicated support, because they're more centralized, so that's where I was more focused, was what does that process look like, so….
Yeah, I think that process in this case would look like they all have access to the Smartsheet, whether it's a Christie, or a NOAA, or a property manager, or someone in between.
We could get them all access to the Smartsheet, and then they enter that information, and as soon as we get that information entered in the Smartsheet, we take it over from there.
Yeah, yeah, we'll definitely keep it at the corporate level.
Don't want the side teams going in there, but, yeah, we can talk through more of those logistics.
I guess good transition into….
And the, ….
the next aspect, which would be probably social, I don't know if you want to briefly touch on that, but if not, we can just go into a little bit more on the pricing as well.
Yeah, we can definitely touch on social.
Essentially, what we're doing with social, it's a similar concept to the posts on Google, where fully automated, direct syndication from the feeds, how we're getting this information.
We're posting property-specific posts onto Instagram, Facebook.
And then we are also layering in, like, holiday posts, pet policy, book a tour now.
So it's 2 posts a week on each platform, fully automated.
So, customers have told us that they like this tool, like, in addition to the Google tool, because it's a similar concept.
Automate it, save your team time, one less thing that they have to do.
Yeah, so just two quick questions I have on this one is, because I haven't seen it in action yet, rent used to offer this, but the issue that we had with them was.
it was basically a billboard for rent.
Like, they would have a little thing on there showcasing this is from Rent.
com.
Like, I want to make sure that that's not the case here.
Doesn't seem to be based on this example, but I want to make sure that's not the case.
And then secondly, outside of the content that's on the website, how are we ensuring, brand co… like, cohesiveness and ensuring that we're not just leveraging standard stock images?
That don't necessarily apply to that target demographic that, like.
What's the quality assurance process there for sites that are leveraging this?
So we are… we're not using this to advertise for Maven AI, so all of the content that we're posting is about the property, tailored to your property.
The intention is for it to look just like it came from a leasing member on your team.
Cool.
And then the photos and the content that we're pulling, it's from the feed first, so that's, like, the source of truth for all of us is directly from the feed.
That's where we're getting the photos, amenity-based information.

Our implementation team also does look at property websites during implementation, in case there's additional photography or amenity-based information that isn't in the feed, they'll layer on what's on the website.
And then the only stock photos that we're using right now would be for, like, holiday posts, or to highlight a pet policy.
That is optional, though.
If it's… if you're just like, we just don't want any stock photos, we could just opt those properties out of that.
Okay.
Yeah, that's something that I would love to build out a little bit more before we, like, take it national scale, is, like.
You know, having, like, a set gallery of photography that people can select from, or, you know, like.

Hey, this is our urban, you know, whatever, like, you know, high-rise, like, demographic, or, like, more, traditional family-oriented building, like, and they have certain imagery and photography that speaks to that target demographic that we can leverage.

Because, like, a lot of these sites only have so much, you know, content when it comes to photography or renderings or whatever, that what I don't want is just have redundancy after the first 2 or 3, you know, rounds of posting, where you're done just… the AI is just picking up the same stuff and reposting it over and over again.
Outside of the holiday posts, of course.
So, I would just love to, like, talk through it a little bit more, but that could be a separate call from this, right?
So, just something to think about.
Yeah, and those are all things we can do.
So, just like the Google tool, this is built in-house as well, which gives us the ability to be flexible with it, customize it at the property level.
We can do things on the back end of this, like customize your brand voice at a property level as well.
So, there's definitely levers and layers of this that we can make it sound and feel more like your property does.
Cool.
Any other questions on the social piece before we jump into pricing?
Not for me.
Don't mind.
Alright.
Well, to jump into pricing, so we have, come up with the custom price at a property level for asset, so that is $99 per property for the whole Google tool that we went over today.
And then, we also came up with a per property price for the social tool.
So, that's $49 a property, add-on to the Google tool.
What is your, sort of, Rack rate pricing, if, you know.
small client comes to you and wants to have this on a couple other properties.
Are they… are they getting $99, or what does that typically look like?
No, the $99 is typically our enterprise, pricing.
Our standard price is $199 per property per month, so that's where it starts for, like, smaller portfolios that have a handful of properties, and then we scale it down from there.
Okay, great.
And so… What does this look like if we go to… 200 properties and, you know, 500 properties, what does it look like from there?
Yeah, I think we're open to that, David.

So, the background of the 99 was, I believe we talked to last year, was Brent and a few others members on the team, and the consensus was, hey, just give us, like, the enterprise portfolio level price, and we'll take it and get buy-in from there.
And so that's what we did.
We went to 99.

However, if we get to a point, and maybe that'll be the next call, we're like, hey, we'd like to see 200, 500, 700 plus property tiering, we're open to working around and adjusting for more bulk discounts as well, off the 99.
Perfect.
Yeah, I think, you know, it's something Saad and I can talk about on where we think this could be a fit in our portfolio.
… I don't know if you guys are aware, we just acquired another management company, so… Crazy.
And did you guys work with FPI at all?
Yep.
Okay.
Great.
Not to the scale we are here, but we know their marketing team.
Yeah.
But that's… big congrats, by the way, that's awesome, that's a huge move.
Yeah, thank you, we're excited.

So, I think it's something Saad and I can talk about, and talk to their team, and figure out where do we think this is an opportunity to roll it out, but it does seem like there's a good opportunity for us to try to scale this.
Yeah, and… you know, obviously, we are at 99 now for Google.
Google is the flagship product that's been around for 4 years.

We added social on a little under a year ago, so, … I think we wanted to obviously hit on the… we can support large-scale rollouts, and then it's just a matter of, hey, let's work through If 99… If there's a bulk scale option for you, off 99, what that could look like across the board, so….
Yeah, I think that'd be good to look at, and you know, if your form MSA, I can take a look at how that's structured.
And then I can regroup with Saad and team on… how much of an opportunity we think there is, but either way, at 100 properties, we should have an MSA in place that's….
Yeah.
you know, I'm generally a kind of… 40, 50 properties is probably where we want to start looking at having an MSA, since it's a bigger partnership at that level.
Yep.
Yeah, I'll send over the order form that we have for those 60 properties, and then if there's anything else you need to see from us, just let me know.
Perfect.
And then, Saad, I think from here… oh, go ahead, Saad.
No, go ahead.
I'll say, I think from here, like, us and our team will get together on just putting maybe 3 options in front of you for scale.
Yeah.
Looking at the 99 as kind of the rack rate, and then moving upwards from there, and we can maybe reconvene on that.
I think that'd be great.
Yeah.
Okay.
Yeah, and then I know the pricing for the listing management is not in here, but if you want to just… potentially put that if that's needed as well.

Yeah, the reputation management, we shouldn't… we shouldn't need to leverage, but it doesn't hurt to, like, have that pricing as well, just as a la carte, because you know, maybe this package makes more sense for some of our properties that don't have a lot of cash flow, like, if we can combine everything into one, and they're not even paying for any reputation management, but now they get all these things, you know, for whatever rate that we come up with, so it doesn't hurt to have that as the added price as well, so….
Yeah, sounds good.
We'll definitely put some options together and get that sent over, today.
Cool.
Awesome, guys.
Well, thank you so much for your time, and Kylie and Morgan, I'm sure I'll be talking to you here in a few hours again with poetic, so, look forward to it.
Sounds good.
Thanks so much.
David, good to see you again.
Yep, likewise, and great to meet you.
Take care.
Bye.
